2603.22714.title: PopResume: Causal Fairness Evaluation of LLM/VLM Resume Screeners with Population-Representative Dataset
2603.22714.paper_summary: We present PopResume, a population-representative resume dataset for causal fairness auditing of LLM- and VLM-based resume screening systems. Unlike existing benchmarks that rely on manually injected demographic information and outcome-level disparities, PopResume is grounded in population statistics and preserves natural attribute relationships, enabling path-specific effect (PSE)-based fairness evaluation. We decompose the effect of a protected attribute on resume scores into two paths: the business necessity path, mediated by job-relevant qualifications, and the redlining path, mediated by demographic proxies. This distinction allows auditors to separate legally permissible from impermissible sources of disparity. Evaluating four LLMs and four VLMs on PopResume's 60.8K resumes across five occupations, we identify five representative discrimination patterns that aggregate metrics fail to capture. Our results demonstrate that PSE-based evaluation reveals fairness issues masked by outcome-level measures, underscoring the need for causally-grounded auditing frameworks in AI-assisted hiring.
2603.22714.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了PopResume，一個具有代表性的履歷數據集，用於對基於LLM和VLM的履歷篩選系統進行因果公平性審計。與依賴手動注入的人口統計信息和結果層面差異的現有基準不同，PopResume基於人口統計數據，並保留自然屬性關係，從而實現基於路徑特定效應（PSE）的公平性評估。我們將受保護屬性對履歷分數的影響分解為兩條路徑：商業必要性路徑，通過與工作相關的資格進行中介，以及紅線路徑，通過人口統計代理進行中介。這一區分使審計人員能夠將法律允許的差異來源與不允許的來源分開。在PopResume的60.8K份履歷中，針對五個職業評估了四個LLM和四個VLM，我們識別出五種代表性的歧視模式，而聚合指標無法捕捉到這些模式。我們的結果表明，基於PSE的評估揭示了被結果層面指標掩蓋的公平性問題，強調了在AI輔助招聘中需要基於因果的審計框架。
2603.22714.author: Sumin Yu et.al.
2603.22714.authors: Sumin Yu, Juhyeon Park, Taesup Moon
